text_blocks.text: After the revocation of the Edict of Nantes,
the Dutch Republic received the largest group of
Huguenot refugees, an estimated total of 75,000 to
Many came from the region of the Cevennes, for
instance, the village of Fraissinet-de-Lozere. This was
a huge influx as the entire population of the Dutch
Republic amounted to ca. 2 million at that time.

In 1705, Amsterdam and the area of West Frisia
were the first areas to provide full citizens rights
to Huguenot immigrants, followed by the Dutch
Republic in 1715. Huguenots intermarried with Dutch
